judgmentZh: 巽：小亨，利有攸往，利见大人。
judgmentEn: The Gentle. Success through what is small. It furthers one to have somewhere to go. It furthers one to see the great man.
imageZh: 随风，巽；君子以申命行事。
imageEn: Winds following one upon the other: the image of the Gently Penetrating. Thus the superior man spreads his commands abroad and carries out his undertakings.
interpretationZh: 巽卦象征风，柔顺而渗透。两风相随，无孔不入，以柔克刚，循序渐进方能成事。
interpretationEn: Xun symbolizes wind and gentle penetration. Two winds following each other penetrate everywhere. Gentle persistence achieves what force cannot.

lines.textEn: Six at the beginning: In advancing and in retreating, the perseverance of a warrior furthers.
lines.interpretationZh: 进退犹豫不决，宜以武人般的坚毅果断来行事。
lines.interpretationEn: Hesitating between advance and retreat. The resolve of a warrior is needed.
lines.detailZh: “进退，利武人之贞。”初六以阴居下，处巽之始，最易犹疑不定，想前进又想后退，故有进退失据之象。此爻提醒人在局势初开时，若过度顾虑、柔而无断，便难以入局。所谓“利武人之贞”，并非鼓励粗暴，而是强调需借刚决之气来定其心志，以果断守正补救优柔。对现实而言，此爻告诫人在起步阶段要先立场明确、原则清楚，否则再多技巧也会因摇摆而失效。
lines.detailEn: “Advancing and retreating. It is beneficial for the perseverance of a warrior.” The first line is yielding at the bottom of the hexagram, at the beginning of penetration. Because it lacks firmness, it easily falls into hesitation — wanting to move forward yet shrinking back at once. This creates the image of uncertainty and loss of momentum. The mention of the “warrior” does not praise aggression, but points to the need for decisiveness, discipline, and courage to steady one’s purpose. In practical terms, this line warns that an overly timid start can ruin an otherwise workable plan. At the beginning of any undertaking, one must clarify position and commit inwardly; otherwise gentleness degenerates into wavering.

lines.textZh: 九二：巽在床下，用史巫纷若，吉无咎。
lines.textEn: Nine in the second place: Penetration under the bed. Priests and magicians are used in great number. Good fortune. No blame.
lines.interpretationZh: 谦卑至极如伏于床下，借助祝史巫觋之力，吉利无咎。
lines.interpretationEn: Extreme humility, penetrating beneath the bed. Employing many intermediaries brings fortune.
lines.detailZh: “巽在床下，用史巫纷若，吉无咎。”九二居中得位，能柔顺而守其中。床下为隐微之处，象征深入幽隐、察知未显的问题；“史巫纷若”则有反复祝告、审慎求明之意。此爻并非神秘化，而是说处理复杂局面时，要放下表面姿态，深入细节、广泛求证，借助专业人士、制度流程或经验判断来辨明方向。因其得中，故虽入于隐处，仍能吉而无咎。现代可理解为做调研、咨询顾问、复盘数据，在不张扬处找到真正的突破口。
lines.detailEn: “Penetration beneath the bed. Using scribes and shamans in great number. Good fortune, no blame.” The second line is firm yet centered, making it one of the most balanced positions in the hexagram. “Beneath the bed” suggests entering hidden places — not merely what is seen on the surface, but what lies underneath: motives, overlooked risks, subtle causes. The reference to scribes and shamans can be understood as consulting those with specialized knowledge, ritual authority, or interpretive skill in order to clarify uncertainty. This line is less about superstition than about careful inquiry. Because the line is central and correct in spirit, its descent into hidden matters is not degrading but fruitful. In modern terms, it favors research, diagnosis, confidential investigation, expert consultation, and thoughtful review before action.

lines.textEn: Nine in the third place: Repeated penetration. Humiliation.
lines.interpretationZh: 反复屈从，过于卑顺，有所困窘。
lines.interpretationEn: Repeated submission becomes humiliating. Excessive yielding brings shame.
lines.detailZh: “频巽，吝。”九三过刚而处下卦之上，欲入而未安，故有一再委顺、一再试探之象。“频巽”表示反复低头、不断退让，却未必出于真正明智，往往是内心不定、见风转舵。故曰“吝”，有局促、可惜、失体之意。此爻警示：柔顺若失去原则与节度，就会变成讨好与摇摆，不仅损害自尊，也难获真正信任。在现实关系和职场中，一味察言观色、频繁改口，反而让人觉得不可靠。
lines.detailEn: “Repeated submission brings humiliation.” The third line is firm but placed at the top of the lower trigram, where it presses upward without stable footing. This creates the image of repeated yielding, repeated adjustment, and repeated attempts to conform. Yet such flexibility is not always wise; often it reflects insecurity, people-pleasing, or opportunism. Therefore the text says “shame” or “regretful meanness.” The warning is sharp: gentleness without inner center becomes servility. In relationships or professional life, constantly changing one’s stance in order to satisfy others may seem adaptive, but it erodes dignity and credibility. Xun requires steady penetration, not nervous compliance.

lines.textEn: Six in the fourth place: Remorse vanishes. During the hunt three kinds of game are caught.
lines.interpretationZh: 悔恨消失，如田猎获得三种猎物，收获丰厚。
lines.interpretationEn: Remorse vanishes. Three kinds of game are caught in the hunt. Rich rewards.
lines.detailZh: “悔亡，田获三品。”六四以阴居阴，得位而近九五，能顺承上意，又能得中层执行之实。田猎获三品，象征收获丰富且分层有序，说明此前的疑虑、懊悔已经消除，努力开始转化为实际成果。此爻常被视为巽卦中较佳的位置之一：柔而得位，善于承接、协同、落实，因此能够把无形的命令转化成可见的成绩。现实中意味着项目进入收获期，特别适合团队执行、资源整合、渠道铺设，因方法得当而见成效。
lines.detailEn: “Regret vanishes. In the hunt, three kinds of game are obtained.” The fourth line is yielding in a yielding place and stands close to the ruler in the fifth line. Because of this, it is well positioned to receive guidance and carry it into practice. The image of obtaining “three kinds of game” in the hunt suggests abundant, ordered, and graded results — not a random gain, but a substantial harvest. Earlier uncertainty gives way to effective execution. This is one of the more favorable lines of the hexagram because softness here is properly placed, responsive, and useful. In modern application, it indicates successful implementation, coordinated teamwork, and tangible reward after sustained effort.

lines.textZh: 九五：贞吉悔亡，无不利。无初有终。先庚三日，后庚三日，吉。
lines.textEn: Nine in the fifth place: Perseverance brings good fortune. Remorse vanishes. Nothing that does not further. No beginning, but an end. Before the change, three days. After the change, three days. Good fortune.
lines.interpretationZh: 坚守正道则吉，悔恨消失。虽无好的开始但有好的结局，变革前后各需三日准备。
lines.interpretationEn: Perseverance brings fortune and remorse vanishes. No good beginning but a good end. Allow time before and after changes.
lines.detailZh: “贞吉悔亡，无不利。无初有终，先庚三日，后庚三日，吉。”九五为全卦主爻，刚健中正而居尊位，是以柔道行大事的典范。“无初有终”说明起初未必顺利，甚至方向需经调整，但只要守正不变，终能成事。“先庚三日，后庚三日”历来多解，大意皆指变革前后都需审慎筹谋、反复整顿，不可仓促。此爻揭示巽之最高境界：不是软弱附和，而是在中正原则下，以周密筹划、持续沟通、温和推进完成改革与治理。故其吉最为完备。
lines.detailEn: “Perseverance brings good fortune; regret vanishes. Nothing is unfavorable. There is no beginning, yet there is an end. Three days before geng, three days after geng — good fortune.” The fifth line is the sovereign line of the hexagram: firm, central, correct, and elevated. It shows the highest form of Xun — governing or leading through principled gentleness. “No beginning, yet there is an end” suggests that matters may not start smoothly; plans may need revision or timing may initially be unclear. Yet with steadfast correctness, completion is achieved. The phrase about ‘three days before and after geng’ points to careful preparation and follow-through around moments of change. Reform cannot be improvised. This line teaches that the greatest influence comes from combining clarity of principle with patient, thorough, and humane implementation.

lines.textZh: 上九：巽在床下，丧其资斧，贞凶。
lines.textEn: Nine at the top: Penetration under the bed. He loses his property and his ax. Perseverance brings misfortune.
lines.interpretationZh: 过度卑顺伏于床下，丧失了财物和权柄，坚持下去则凶。
lines.interpretationEn: Excessive submission beneath the bed. Property and authority are lost. Persisting brings misfortune.
lines.detailZh: “巽在床下，丧其资斧，贞凶。”上九居巽之极，入而过深，顺而太甚，以致失其决断与凭藉。床下仍是幽隐之处，但到了上九，已非审慎探微，而是过度退藏、过度屈从。“资斧”象征资财、工具、权柄与行动能力，若一味委曲求全，最终会把立身处世的根本也丧失。故虽自以为守贞，实则凶。此爻告诫人：柔顺必须有界限，深入必须适可而止。若没有骨干，所谓谦逊就会沦为软弱，反失其本。
lines.detailEn: “Penetration beneath the bed. One loses property and axe. Perseverance brings misfortune.” At the top of the hexagram, penetration has gone too far. What was once subtle inquiry now becomes excessive self-effacement, concealment, or subservience. “Beneath the bed” here no longer suggests useful investigation but an unhealthy depth of withdrawal. The loss of “property and axe” symbolizes losing one’s resources, tools, authority, and capacity for decisive action. This is the danger of Xun carried to an extreme: gentleness without boundary. One may think oneself faithful or correct, yet if one keeps yielding beyond what is proper, misfortune follows. The line warns that humility must never cost one’s essential power, dignity, or means of action.

overviewZh: 巽卦为《易经》第五十七卦，上下皆巽，象为风随风入，无形而能浸润万物。其核心精神不在刚猛突破，而在柔顺渗透、循序渐进，以谦逊、耐心与持续影响达成目标。卦辞言“小亨，利有攸往，利见大人”，说明此卦之吉，不在一时强取，而在守正而行、借助贤明之人、顺势推进。巽既有进入、渗透之义，也有顺从、申命之象，提醒人处世不可优柔失主，而应在柔和中藏坚定，在顺势中守原则。此卦适合处理复杂关系、制度执行、长期经营与内外沟通之事。若能以德服人、以理入心，便能积小成大；若一味退让、反复犹疑，则会陷入进退失据、失其所守的困境。
overviewEn: Hexagram 57, Xun, is The Gentle or The Penetrating Wind, formed by Wind above and Wind below. Its image is not forceful conquest but subtle influence: wind enters quietly, reaches everywhere, and transforms without violence. The hexagram teaches that true progress often comes through humility, consistency, tact, and gradual penetration rather than dramatic assertion. The Judgment says, “Small success. It is beneficial to have somewhere to go. It is beneficial to see the great person,” suggesting that advancement is possible when one proceeds with clarity, proper direction, and wise guidance. Xun represents both entering and yielding, but its yielding is not weakness; it is strategic softness rooted in inner firmness. It is highly relevant in situations involving communication, leadership, persuasion, administration, and long-term relationship building. The danger of this hexagram lies in becoming overly compliant, hesitant, or self-effacing. Properly lived, Xun shows how gentleness can become a durable strength that wins trust, aligns people, and carries intentions deep into the fabric of events.
judgmentDetailZh: 卦辞曰：“巽：小亨，利有攸往，利见大人。”《彖传》进一步解释：“重巽以申命，刚巽乎中正而志行，柔皆顺乎刚，是以小亨，利有攸往，利见大人。”所谓“重巽”，是上下皆风，象征命令、理念、风气层层传达，使影响由外而内、由上而下地展开。“刚巽乎中正”，指九二、九五居中得正，以柔顺之道承载坚定之志，因此并非软弱退缩，而是有原则的通达。“柔皆顺乎刚”，则表示整体格局以和顺配合中正之主，所以能获得“小亨”。此卦所说的亨通并不张扬，不是横空而起的大成，而是通过不断申命、反复沟通、修正起点、依循正道，渐入佳境。故“利见大人”，意在提醒人若想让柔顺不流于软弱，必须亲近有德有识之人，以明方向、定法度、成其事。
judgmentDetailEn: The Judgment reads: “Xun: Small success. It is beneficial to have somewhere to go. It is beneficial to see the great person.” The Tuan Commentary expands: “Repeated Xun means reiterating commands. The firm yields in the center and in correctness, and thus its will is carried out. The yielding all follow the firm; therefore there is small success. It is beneficial to have somewhere to go, and beneficial to see the great person.” The idea of “repeated Xun” points to layered penetration: influence spreads by repetition, clarification, and steady implementation rather than sudden domination. The phrase “the firm yields in the center and in correctness” refers to the central yang lines that remain principled while acting with gentleness. This is not weakness, but disciplined flexibility. “The yielding all follow the firm” shows a structure in which softness aligns with a sound core, making progress possible. The “small success” of Xun is modest but real: it arises through refinement, patient communication, and alignment with what is right. Hence the benefit of “seeing the great person” — one needs mature guidance, moral authority, or an exemplary leader so that gentleness does not collapse into indecision. Xun teaches that when principles are quietly but persistently embodied, influence deepens and affairs gradually reach completion.
imageDetailZh: 《象传》曰：“随风，巽；君子以申命行事。”风本无形，却能穿林越谷、入屋入隙，前风未止，后风又随，其势不猛，却最能久远。上下皆巽，便是“随风”之象，意味着教令、德化、制度与言语若能反复申明、持续贯彻，就能像风一样深入人心。君子观此卦，不以一时威压求服从，而以清楚传达、反复确认、身体力行来推动事务。所谓“申命行事”，不是空言号令，而是先使命令被理解，再使行动有次序地落实。故巽卦之象强调执行力来自润物无声的渗透，而非简单强制。在个人修养上，它也提示人应如风般谦下、灵活、通达，同时避免飘忽无定。
imageDetailEn: The Image says, “Winds following one another: Xun. Thus the noble one reiterates commands and carries out affairs.” Wind is invisible, yet it reaches everywhere, entering woods, valleys, houses, and narrow spaces. One wind follows another; the movement is not violent, but it is persistent and difficult to resist. This doubled wind becomes the image of repeated instruction, moral influence, and quiet implementation. The noble person does not rely on intimidation alone, but on clear communication, repeated clarification, and consistent practice. “Reiterating commands and carrying out affairs” means that good governance or effective action depends on more than issuing orders once; intentions must be explained, internalized, and embodied in execution. Thus Xun values continuity over force, and persuasion over harsh pressure. In personal cultivation, it suggests humility, adaptability, and the ability to move through situations with tact while still retaining direction. The warning is that wind without orientation becomes drifting. Therefore the true power of Xun lies in guided gentleness — softness that enters deeply because it is steady, purposeful, and aligned with principle.
modernApplicationZh: 事业上，巽卦适合以协调、沟通、策略推进为主的阶段，尤其利于管理、咨询、销售、教育、品牌传播、组织变革等工作。此时不宜逞强硬碰，而应先理解局势、争取关键支持者，再逐层推进。感情上，巽卦提示关系需要柔和表达与持续沟通，适合修复误会、建立信任，但忌一味迁就而失去边界，真正的温柔应当带着清晰立场。财运上，此卦利于稳健经营、长期布局、以口碑与人脉积累收益，不利于激进投机；财富更可能来自耐心渗透市场、服务客户、优化流程。健康上，宜关注神经系统、肝胆、呼吸系统及情绪压力，过度思虑、压抑和犹疑会耗伤精力。总体而言，巽卦在现代生活中的启示是：先入人心，再成其事；先理顺关系与规则，再谈规模与结果。柔和不是退缩，而是更高明、更可持续的推进方式。
modernApplicationEn: In career matters, Xun favors phases that depend on coordination, communication, diplomacy, and strategic implementation. It is especially helpful for management, consulting, sales, education, branding, and organizational change. This is not the time for blunt confrontation; it is better to read the atmosphere, gain support from key people, and then advance layer by layer. In relationships, Xun points to gentle speech, patience, and ongoing communication. It supports reconciliation and trust-building, but warns against excessive accommodation that erases personal boundaries; healthy gentleness must still contain clarity and self-respect. In finances, the hexagram favors steady management, reputation-based growth, and long-term positioning rather than reckless speculation. Wealth comes more reliably through service, trust, process improvement, and sustained market presence. In health, one should watch stress, nervous tension, breathing balance, liver-gallbladder regulation in traditional terms, and the effects of overthinking or emotional suppression. Overall, Xun teaches a modern lesson: influence before expansion, alignment before acceleration. Lasting success often belongs not to the loudest force, but to the patient presence that enters deeply, adapts intelligently, and keeps moving with principled consistency.
historicalStoryZh: 巽卦常使人联想到周公制礼作乐、反复申命以安天下的治理之道。周初天下新定，殷民未附，诸侯未一，若一味以武力压服，难得长治。周公所采取的并非单纯强制，而是通过礼制、教化、封建秩序与政治安抚，一层层把周的原则、命令与文化渗入诸侯与民间。其道正合“随风，巽；君子以申命行事”之义。又如孔子周游列国，虽屡遭不遇，却始终以温言明理、因人施教，传播仁礼之道，也体现了巽卦“柔而能入”的精神。巽不是立刻改天换地，而是以持久、细密、反复而不暴烈的方式，改变人心与秩序。
historicalStoryEn: Xun may be illustrated by the political wisdom of the Duke of Zhou. In the early Zhou dynasty, the realm had only recently been settled; former Shang subjects were unconvinced, and the feudal lords were not yet fully aligned. Mere military coercion could suppress resistance, but not secure lasting order. The Duke of Zhou therefore relied on rites, music, moral education, political reassurance, and carefully structured institutions to transmit Zhou authority step by step into the hearts of both nobles and common people. This embodies the Image of Xun: “The noble one reiterates commands and carries out affairs.” One may also think of Confucius traveling among the states, not conquering by force, but teaching through patient explanation, moral example, and tailored guidance. Xun does not usually transform the world overnight. Its way is subtler: repeated influence, quiet penetration, and durable shaping of character and order. Through this, what seems soft can become one of the strongest powers in history.
relatedHexagramsNote: 巽与兑皆重卦而主交流，但兑重在悦人、以说感通，巽重在入人、以渐渗透；与震相对，震主发动惊起，巽主柔入申命。理解巽卦，可与震、兑对读：震开局，巽推进，兑成和。
references: 《彖传》：‘重巽以申命，刚巽乎中正而志行，柔皆顺乎刚。’
《象传》：‘随风，巽；君子以申命行事。’

judgmentEn: The Joyous. Success. Perseverance is favorable.
imageZh: 丽泽，兑；君子以朋友讲习。
imageEn: Lakes resting one on the other: the image of the Joyous. Thus the superior man joins with his friends for discussion and practice.
interpretationZh: 兑卦象征泽，喜悦而和谐。两泽相连，互相滋润，以喜悦之心待人，朋友切磋共进。
interpretationEn: Dui symbolizes the lake and joyousness. Two lakes connected nourish each other. Joy shared with friends through mutual learning and encouragement.

lines.textEn: Nine at the beginning: Contented joyousness. Good fortune.
lines.interpretationZh: 和谐的喜悦，内心平和而快乐，吉利。
lines.interpretationEn: Harmonious joy from within. Contentment brings good fortune.
lines.detailZh: “和兑，吉。”初九居下卦之始，以阳刚而处卑位，不争先，不矜才，其悦来自内心平和而非外在取宠，所以吉。此爻说明最好的喜悦，是自然、谦和、无机心的相处方式。处在人际关系起点时，若能先求和气与真诚，往往最能赢得信任。
lines.detailEn: “Harmonious joy. Good fortune.” At the beginning of the hexagram, this yang line is strong yet modestly placed. It does not force itself forward or seek attention. Its joy is calm, natural, and free of calculation, therefore auspicious. The line teaches that the best kind of joy begins in sincerity and peaceful conduct rather than in trying to impress others.

lines.textEn: Nine in the second place: Sincere joyousness. Good fortune. Remorse disappears.
lines.interpretationZh: 以诚信带来的喜悦，吉利，悔恨消失。
lines.interpretationEn: Joy grounded in sincerity. Good fortune and remorse vanishes.
lines.detailZh: “孚兑，吉，悔亡。”九二得中，为内卦主位，所示之悦以诚信为本。“孚”即真实可信，意味着只有让人感到可托付、可相信，喜悦才不会流于表面。故其结果是吉，且过去的懊悔与隔阂也能消解。这一爻尤其利于修复关系、重建信任。
lines.detailEn: “Joy of sincerity. Good fortune. Regret vanishes.” This central yang line occupies the proper inner position, showing balanced and trustworthy joy. ‘Fu’ means sincerity and credibility. When joy is rooted in what is genuine, it is not superficial charm but something others can safely respond to. Such sincerity dissolves regret and heals previous strains in relationship.

lines.textEn: Six in the third place: Coming joyousness. Misfortune.
lines.interpretationZh: 刻意追求外来的快乐，迎合取悦，凶险。
lines.interpretationEn: Seeking joy from external sources. Pandering to pleasure brings misfortune.
lines.detailZh: “来兑，凶。”六三以阴柔失正，又居内卦之上，容易主动迎合、追逐欢悦，表现出向外索取认可的倾向。所谓“来兑”，即刻意奔赴取悦之境，反失其本，因此凶。此爻警示：若把快乐建立在讨好、依附、迎合上，短期虽有热闹，长期必致失衡与受制。
lines.detailEn: “Coming joy. Misfortune.” This yin line is improperly placed and tends toward seeking joy from outside rather than resting in inner truth. ‘Coming joy’ suggests running after approval, pleasure, or pleasing interaction in a deliberate, dependent way. Because it abandons inner balance for external gratification, the result is unfavorable.

lines.textZh: 九四：商兑未宁，介疾有喜。
lines.textEn: Nine in the fourth place: Joyousness that is weighed is not at peace. After ridding himself of faults a man has joy.
lines.interpretationZh: 权衡取舍尚未安宁，去除弊病之后方有喜悦。
lines.interpretationEn: Deliberating over pleasures brings no peace. Joy comes after eliminating faults.
lines.detailZh: “商兑未宁，介疾有喜。”九四处上下之间，进退未定，故有商量斟酌、内心不安之象。“介疾”可理解为有所警觉，如小病般令人收敛反思。正因能察觉问题、不放纵于表面之悦，最终反有喜庆。此爻表明，在复杂关系中，适度犹疑与警惕并非坏事，反而能帮助人避险得安。
lines.detailEn: “Negotiating joy, not yet at rest. A minor affliction brings happiness.” Positioned between the inner and outer trigram, this line suggests uncertainty, deliberation, and incomplete ease. The ‘small illness’ symbolizes a warning or discomfort that checks excess. Because the person does not abandon caution for easy pleasure, this restraint ultimately leads to a positive outcome.

lines.textEn: Nine in the fifth place: Sincerity toward disintegrating influences is dangerous.
lines.interpretationZh: 信任那些有害的事物，有危险。对小人的信任会带来灾祸。
lines.interpretationEn: Trusting in destructive influences is dangerous. Beware of misplaced sincerity.
lines.detailZh: “孚于剥，有厉。”九五居尊位，本应中正主持喜悦之道，但此时却对“剥”而信，即信任正在侵蚀正道之人或事，故有危厉。此爻特别提醒身居高位者：不可被甜言、表面忠顺或短期利益所迷。若把信任交给会剥蚀根本的人，危险虽未立刻爆发，却已潜伏。
lines.detailEn: “Sincerity toward what is stripping away. There is danger.” In the ruling position, this line should govern joy with clarity, yet it places trust in forces that erode the good. This points to the danger of believing flattering voices, corrosive influences, or relationships that undermine one’s foundations. For leaders especially, misplaced trust creates serious risk.

lines.textEn: Six at the top: Seductive joyousness.
lines.interpretationZh: 引诱他人的喜悦，沉溺于享乐，被欲望牵引。
lines.interpretationEn: Seductive joy that leads others astray. Drawn along by desire and indulgence.
lines.detailZh: “引兑。”上六居悦之极，阴柔在上，有牵引他人同悦之象，也有被欢悦之境反向牵引之意。若无正守，容易沉溺于交际、言辞、情感依赖之中，难以自拔。此爻是兑卦终点的提醒：快乐到了极处，最需节制与抽身，否则喜悦反成牵累。
lines.detailEn: “Drawn into joy.” At the top of the hexagram, joy reaches its extreme. This line suggests either drawing others into pleasure or being oneself drawn along by it. Without self-command, one can become entangled in social charm, emotional dependency, or endless pursuit of agreeable feeling. The line warns that excessive joy easily turns into attachment.

overviewZh: 兑卦为《易经》第五十八卦，上下皆兑，象为重泽，名曰“兑”，意为喜悦、和悦、沟通与感应。此卦所呈现的，并非轻浮嬉乐，而是建立在正固与诚信基础上的悦服之道。卦辞曰“亨，利贞”，说明真正的喜悦可以通达上下、润泽人心，但前提是守持正道，不可流于逢迎取媚。兑为口，也主言说，因此此卦特别强调语言的力量：善言能和众、通志、解结，恶言则可惑众、伤德、致祸。整体来看，兑卦提示人在群体关系、朋友往来、教化学习与社会交往中，应以诚感人、以和导人、以正节情。它既象征内外气氛的融洽，也提醒人们警惕表面愉悦之下的空虚与诱惑。故兑之智慧，在于以正养悦、以信立悦、以节制守悦，使快乐成为成德之源，而非失德之端。
overviewEn: Hexagram 58, Dui, is The Joyous, formed by Lake over Lake. Its image is that of two marshes reflecting and nourishing each other, symbolizing joy, communication, responsiveness, and shared understanding. Yet the joy of Dui is not shallow amusement or pleasing words for their own sake. The Judgment says, “Success. Perseverance is favorable,” indicating that true joy becomes beneficial only when rooted in integrity and moral steadiness. Dui is also associated with the mouth and speech, so this hexagram highlights the power of words: sincere speech can unite people, ease tensions, and inspire learning, while manipulative or seductive talk can mislead and corrupt. In human affairs, Dui points to friendship, teaching, mutual encouragement, and harmonious exchange. It encourages one to cultivate an atmosphere in which people can communicate openly and learn from one another. At the same time, it warns against empty charm, indulgence, and emotional dependence on approval. The deeper wisdom of Dui lies in joy disciplined by truth, relationship guided by sincerity, and pleasure balanced by principle.
judgmentDetailZh: 卦辞“兑：亨，利贞”言简义深，指出悦并非次要情绪，而是一种能使万事流通的德性力量。但这种“亨”不是纵情享乐所得，而是“利贞”所保障。彖传说：“兑，说也。刚中而柔外，说以利贞，是以顺乎天而应乎人。说以先民，民忘其劳；说以犯难，民忘其死；说之大，民劝矣哉。”此处明示，兑之本质在“说”，即悦服、喜悦。内有刚中，表示内心有原则、有主心骨；外见柔和，表示待人温润、言辞不迫。因为内刚外柔，所以这种喜悦既不僵硬，也不轻佻，能够上顺天理，下应人情。一个领导者若能以诚信和正道使人悦服，百姓便能忘劳忘死，乐于追随。落到个人修养，兑卦要求人说话有分寸，交往有真诚，施教有启发，待人有和气。若离开“贞”，则喜悦会堕为阿谀、享乐、诱惑，终致凶危。因此此卦的核心，不在“讨好”，而在以正道形成可持续的和悦秩序。
judgmentDetailEn: The Judgment, “Dui: Success. Perseverance is favorable,” reveals that joy is not a trivial emotion but a force that can open and harmonize human affairs. However, its success depends on perseverance in what is right. The Tuan Commentary says: “Dui means joy. Firm within and yielding without, joy that is favorable through perseverance—thereby one accords with Heaven and responds to people. By joy one leads the people, and they forget their toil; by joy one faces danger, and they forget death. Great indeed is the power of joy to encourage the people.” This passage makes the structure of the hexagram morally clear. Its inner firmness means inner principle, conviction, and clarity; its outer softness means graciousness, approachability, and gentle expression. When inner strength is combined with outer openness, joy becomes trustworthy rather than frivolous. It is then able to resonate both with higher principle and with human feeling. In leadership, education, and daily relationships, Dui teaches that sincere encouragement is more powerful than coercion. Yet it also warns that joy cut off from uprightness degenerates into flattery, indulgence, or seduction. The heart of the hexagram is therefore not people-pleasing, but the creation of lasting harmony through truthful and principled delight.
imageDetailZh: 象辞曰：“丽泽，兑；君子以朋友讲习。”所谓“丽泽”，是两泽并列，相互映照、彼此滋润。单一之泽有限，重泽则气象开阔，象征人与人之间通过交流而增益德业。兑为悦，也为口，故其象不只在自然景观，更在精神互动：朋友之间切磋学问、辨析义理、互相砥砺，正是兑之象的现实展开。君子观此，不闭门自守，而是通过讲论与学习拓展见识，通过和悦气氛促成共同成长。这里强调的不是空谈，而是有内容、有诚意、有启发的“讲习”。真正的交流应如双泽相通，彼此成全，而非互相消耗。故兑卦之象提示我们：健康的人际关系，应建立在开放、信任与共同精进之上；悦不是表面的热闹，而是使人心舒展、使德行润泽的力量。
imageDetailEn: The Image says, “Lakes joined together: the image of Dui. Thus the superior person joins with friends for discussion and practice.” The phrase suggests two bodies of water connected, reflecting and nourishing one another. A single lake is limited; two lakes together create exchange, circulation, and mutual enrichment. This image becomes a model for human relationships. Dui is joy, but also speech, so the hexagram points to the way understanding deepens through open conversation. The noble person does not isolate himself in private certainty. Instead, he studies with others, refines insight through dialogue, and grows through shared practice. The emphasis is not on idle talk but on meaningful discussion rooted in sincerity and learning. Just as joined lakes support one another, friends and colleagues should strengthen rather than drain each other. The image therefore presents joy as something relational and educative: a field of trust where minds are sharpened, hearts are eased, and virtue is nourished through mutual exchange.
modernApplicationZh: 在现代生活中，兑卦最重要的启示，是把“愉悦的关系”建立在真实、边界与正直之上。事业方面，兑提示沟通力、协调力、表达力就是生产力。适合谈判、教学、咨询、销售、内容传播等领域，但须防只讲场面话、只顾短期讨好，导致团队信任流失。感情方面，兑主互动甜美、相处轻松，利于修复隔阂、增强表达与倾听，但若沉迷情绪满足、回避深层问题，也容易形成表面和谐、内里空虚。财运方面，兑有利于依靠口才、人脉、合作、品牌形象得财，尤其适合服务业与社交型商业；但必须谨防娱乐消费过度、轻信诱导、因贪图享受而破财。健康方面，兑对应口腔、呼吸、情绪系统，也提醒心理压力常被“笑着掩盖”，所以要重视真实表达、规律作息和舒缓社交疲劳。总的说来，兑卦不是鼓励一味追求快乐，而是教人经营一种可持续的舒心状态：说真话，交益友，守正道，在和悦中成事，在节制中长久。
modernApplicationEn: In modern life, the central lesson of Hexagram 58 is to build pleasant relationships on truth, boundaries, and integrity. In career matters, Dui emphasizes communication, persuasion, and emotional intelligence. It is favorable for negotiation, teaching, counseling, sales, media, and any work that depends on trust and verbal influence. Yet one must avoid empty rhetoric or short-term pleasing behavior that gradually erodes credibility. In love and relationships, Dui supports warmth, lightness, reconciliation, and honest conversation. It helps people reconnect through listening and emotional openness. Still, if one chases pleasant feelings while avoiding deeper issues, the relationship may become outwardly sweet but inwardly unstable. In finances, Dui can bring gains through networking, partnerships, branding, and service-oriented industries, but it also warns against overspending on pleasure, social image, or impulsive consumption. In health, Dui is linked with the mouth, lungs, and emotional regulation; it reminds us that stress is sometimes hidden behind a smiling face. The remedy is authentic expression, balanced social life, and disciplined enjoyment. Dui teaches not the pursuit of pleasure at any cost, but the art of sustaining well-being through sincere speech, worthy companionship, and joy kept within ethical measure.
historicalStoryZh: 若以历史故事比兑卦之义，可联想到孔子与弟子讲学、与朋友切磋的场景。孔门之学并非板滞说教，而是在问答、讨论、反省中展开，形成“有朋自远方来，不亦乐乎”的气象。这种“乐”并非宴饮嬉游，而是因道相契、因学相长而生的内在喜悦，正合兑卦“朋友讲习”之象。又如战国纵横家以言辞游说诸侯，亦可见兑为口、为说服之力；但其中有正有邪，有的以信义通天下，有的以巧言惑人心，成败高下遂判然有别。由此可知，语言确能成事，也能败事；喜悦与说服若有诚信节义为本，则能团结人心、转危为机，若仅凭机巧取悦，则终难持久。兑卦借“悦”示人，最终所重者，仍是德行与分寸。
historicalStoryEn: A fitting historical illustration of Dui can be found in the teaching community of Confucius and his disciples. The joy of that tradition was not mere entertainment, but the delight of shared learning, questioning, and moral refinement. The saying, “To have friends come from afar—is this not a joy?” beautifully echoes the Image of Dui: friends gathering for discussion and practice. Their joy arose from resonance in the Way and from mutual growth. A contrasting example appears in the persuaders of the Warring States period, whose eloquence could sway rulers and shape events. There, too, we see Dui as speech and influence. Some used words grounded in loyalty and principle; others relied on clever manipulation. The difference determined whether their influence endured or collapsed. These examples show the double edge of Dui: language can unite hearts or deceive them, joy can nourish virtue or conceal corruption. What makes joy beneficial is not charm alone, but sincerity, moral measure, and a clear center.
relatedHexagramsNote: 兑卦与第57卦巽卦常可对观：巽为入，为渐进渗透；兑为说，为喜悦感通。巽重影响方式，兑重表达效果。又可与第31卦咸卦参照，咸言感应，兑言和悦，二者皆关乎人际互动。理解这些联系，有助于把握“悦而不失正”的分寸。
references: 《周易》：兑卦卦辞、爻辞、象传、彖传

judgmentZh: 涣：亨。王假有庙，利涉大川，利贞。
judgmentEn: Dispersion. Success. The king approaches his temple. It furthers one to cross the great water. Perseverance furthers.
imageZh: 风行水上，涣；先王以享于帝立庙。
imageEn: The wind drives over the water: the image of Dispersion. Thus the kings of old sacrificed to the Lord and built temples.
interpretationZh: 涣卦象征涣散，风行水上，波浪四散。以祭祀凝聚人心，以大义化解离散，方能重新团结。
interpretationEn: Huan symbolizes dispersion. Wind blowing over water scatters it. Unite scattered hearts through shared devotion and higher purpose.

lines.textEn: Six at the beginning: He brings help with the strength of a horse. Good fortune.
lines.interpretationZh: 用壮马之力来拯救，及时施救则吉利。
lines.interpretationEn: Rescue comes with the strength of a horse. Swift aid brings good fortune.
lines.detailZh: “用拯马壮，吉。”初六居下，象征涣散刚起之时，局面虽乱，但尚可及时挽救。所谓“拯”，重在迅速援助；“马壮”则象征有力的行动工具与执行力。此爻告诫人们，在问题刚露端倪时，不可迟疑观望，应立刻借助可靠力量稳定局面。越是初乱，越要果断。若能及时动员人手、资源或意志，散局可止，故吉。
lines.detailEn: “Use a strong horse for rescue. Good fortune.” At the beginning of dispersion, disorder has started but can still be contained. “Rescue” stresses timely intervention, while the “strong horse” symbolizes capable means and decisive action. This line teaches that when the first signs of breakdown appear, one should not hesitate. Prompt support, firm mobilization, and energetic response can prevent wider scattering. Because the situation is still recoverable, swift action brings good fortune.

lines.textEn: Nine in the second place: At the dissolution he hurries to that which supports him. Remorse disappears.
lines.interpretationZh: 涣散之时奔向可依靠之处，悔恨消失。
lines.interpretationEn: In times of dispersion, hasten to a place of support. Remorse vanishes.
lines.detailZh: “涣奔其机，悔亡。”九二居内卦中位，得中而应，表示在离散之际应迅速回到安身立命之所。“机”可理解为凭依、坐处、根本。此爻强调，不必在外部混乱中盲目奔走，而要先回归核心位置、关键关系或基础原则。找到支点，悔恨自消。它也提示人在危机中最怕失其所守，只要守住中心，局面仍可转圜。
lines.detailEn: “In dispersion, he hurries to his support. Remorse disappears.” As the central line of the lower trigram, Nine in the second place suggests returning quickly to one’s proper base. “Support” or “resting place” means the core position, the dependable foundation, or the principle one can rely upon. Rather than running blindly amid confusion, one should move back toward what is stable and essential. Once a sound center is regained, regret fades and the situation can be corrected.

lines.textEn: Six in the third place: He dissolves his self. No remorse.
lines.interpretationZh: 化解自身的私欲执着，无所悔恨。
lines.interpretationEn: Dissolving selfish concerns. No remorse when ego is set aside.
lines.detailZh: “涣其躬，无悔。”六三处于内外交界，往往有自我纠结、进退失据之象。此爻指出，先从自身化解，散去私意、执念与防御，便可无悔。许多纷争的根源，并不全在外部，而在于自己心中郁结未开。若能反省自身、放下偏执、调整姿态，就能避免更大失误。涣卦到此，已由外在局势转入内在修正。
lines.detailEn: “He dissolves his self. No remorse.” Positioned at the threshold between inner and outer, this line often reflects tension within the person rather than merely in the environment. Its teaching is that one must first dissolve ego-clinging, defensiveness, and private fixation. Many conflicts persist not only because of outer conditions, but because inner knots remain untied. By softening the self, examining one’s motives, and releasing stubbornness, one avoids regret.

lines.textZh: 六四：涣其群，元吉。涣有丘，匪夷所思。
lines.textEn: Six in the fourth place: He dissolves his bond with his group. Supreme good fortune. Dispersion leads to accumulation. This is something that ordinary men do not think of.
lines.interpretationZh: 化解小团体的私利，大吉。涣散之后重新聚合如丘，非常人所能想到。
lines.interpretationEn: Dissolving factional bonds brings supreme fortune. Dispersion leads to a higher gathering beyond ordinary thinking.
lines.detailZh: “涣其群，元吉。涣有丘，匪夷所思。”六四居近君之位，能够影响群体，是涣卦中极关键的一爻。“涣其群”不是瓦解团队，而是打破小圈子、小集团和彼此隔膜，使原本局促的结党之气散开，从而形成更高层次的整合，所以“大吉”。“涣有丘”言其聚散之后反成高丘般的新中心，其成效非常人所易料。此爻最能体现涣中有聚、散中有成的深意。
lines.detailEn: “He dissolves his group. Supreme good fortune. Dispersion leads to a mound. This is not what one would ordinarily think.” Six in the fourth place is close to authority and can influence the collective. “Dissolving the group” does not mean destroying community; it means breaking up cliques, factional boundaries, and narrow identifications so a higher unity can emerge. Therefore it is greatly auspicious. After unhealthy clustering is dissolved, a new center—like a raised mound—appears. The outcome exceeds ordinary expectation, showing that true dispersion can prepare a deeper gathering.

lines.textZh: 九五：涣汗其大号，涣王居，无咎。
lines.textEn: Nine in the fifth place: His loud cries are as dissolving as sweat. Dissolution! A king abides without blame.
lines.interpretationZh: 如汗水般发布重大号令，王者居正位化解涣散，无咎。
lines.interpretationEn: Great proclamations dissolve barriers like sweat. The king holds his position without blame.
lines.detailZh: “涣汗其大号，涣王居，无咎。”九五为君位，是全卦主爻。“汗其大号”有如号令一出，遍布四方，如汗发于体，畅通无阻，象征政令公开、诚意昭示、上下通达。“涣王居”则表明君主安居中位，以稳定核心化解四方离散。此爻说明，真正的领导不是强行捆绑众人，而是通过正大光明的号召与中正安定的存在，使人心自然归附，因此无咎。
lines.detailEn: “His great command is issued like sweat. The king remains in his dwelling. No blame.” Nine in the fifth place is the ruling line of the hexagram. The image of sweat suggests something spreading naturally through the whole body; likewise, a sincere and open proclamation reaches all levels without obstruction. “The king remains in his place” means the ruler holds the center steadily rather than reacting in panic. This line teaches that effective leadership in times of dispersion does not rely on coercive binding, but on clear authority, moral openness, and calm central presence. Thus there is no blame.

lines.textZh: 上九：涣其血，去逖出，无咎。
lines.textEn: Nine at the top: He dissolves his blood. Departing, keeping at a distance, going out, is without blame.
lines.interpretationZh: 化解血光之灾，远离危险，离去则无咎。
lines.interpretationEn: Dissolving bloodshed by departing and keeping distance. No blame in withdrawal.
lines.detailZh: “涣其血，去逖出，无咎。”上九处涣之终，意味着危险、怨恨或创伤若再不排解，便可能深入成害。“血”象征伤痛、冲突与牵连。此爻主张将最深的危险彻底疏离：远去其害，退出其争，不再让旧伤持续纠缠。它并非逃避，而是在局势已近极端时，果断切断恶性联系，保全自身与大局。能如此清理尾端风险，故无咎。
lines.detailEn: “He disperses his blood. Departing, keeping far away, going out—no blame.” At the top of the hexagram, dispersion reaches its final stage. “Blood” symbolizes injury, conflict, entanglement, and deep emotional danger. This line advises removing the most harmful residue completely: step away, create distance, and leave what has become destructive. This is not cowardly escape but wise severance when a situation has gone too far. By ending toxic involvement and preventing old wounds from deepening, one remains without blame.

overviewZh: 第59卦为涣卦，上巽下坎，风行水上，象征离散、疏解、化解与重新汇聚。所谓“涣”，并不只是瓦解崩散，更强调在固结、闭塞、恐惧或隔阂形成之后，以柔顺而通达的力量将僵局打开，使人心重新流动、关系重新联结、秩序重新建立。此卦告诉人们，当局面陷于分裂时，单靠强压往往无效，必须先化其郁结，再导其归一。卦辞提出“王假有庙”，说明面对离心离德之势，最重要的是回到共同信念、共同根本与共同目标，以中心凝聚众人。故涣卦虽言散，却不是消极溃散，而是先散其壅滞，再成其通达；先解其纷扰，再立其秩序。对个人而言，它意味着放下执念、疏通情绪、打破隔膜；对组织而言，则意味着危机中的整合、重建共识与恢复信任。
overviewEn: Hexagram 59, Huan, consists of Wind above Water, portraying wind moving over the surface of water. It signifies dispersion, dissolution, release, and the restoration of flow. Yet “dispersion” here does not simply mean collapse or fragmentation. Rather, it points to the wise loosening of rigidity, fear, estrangement, and emotional blockage so that vitality can circulate again. When a situation becomes hardened by distrust or confusion, force alone cannot heal it; what is needed is a gentle but penetrating influence that dissolves tension and reconnects what has drifted apart. The Judgment emphasizes that the ruler approaches the ancestral temple, showing that in times of division one must return to a shared center—common values, sacred purpose, and legitimate authority. Thus Huan teaches that true renewal may require first scattering what is stagnant before gathering what is essential. For individuals, it means releasing fixation and clearing the heart. For groups, it suggests rebuilding trust, restoring communication, and transforming disunity into renewed cohesion.
judgmentDetailZh: 卦辞曰：“涣：亨。王假有庙，利涉大川，利贞。”《彖传》说：“涣，亨，刚来而不穷，柔得位乎外而上同。王假有庙，王乃在中也。利涉大川，乘木有功也。”其意在于，涣卦之所以能够“亨”，并非任由散乱蔓延，而是因刚柔得其配置，内有主导，外有顺承，使离散之势可以被引导、被转化。尤其“王乃在中”，说明面对人心分散、局势浮动时，必须有居中而正的核心来主持大局。宗庙在古代不仅是祭祀场所，更是凝聚宗族与国家认同的精神中心，所以“假有庙”象征回归共同根本，以诚敬重新统一人心。“利涉大川”则表明，危机并非不可渡越，只要能借助适当工具与秩序，就能穿越险难。涣卦最终仍归于“利贞”，指出一切疏解与变通都必须立足于正道，否则流散只会沦为失控。
judgmentDetailEn: The Judgment says: “Dispersion. Success. The king approaches his temple. It is favorable to cross the great river. Perseverance is beneficial.” The Tuan Commentary explains: “Dispersion, success, because the firm comes and is not exhausted, and the yielding obtains its place outside and joins above. ‘The king approaches his temple’ means the king is in the center. ‘It is favorable to cross the great river’ means achievement comes by riding on wood.” This reveals that success in Huan does not arise from letting disorder spread unchecked. It comes from a proper balance of strength and flexibility, where there is inner leadership and outer responsiveness. The phrase “the king is in the center” is crucial: in times of emotional scattering and social instability, a centered and legitimate core is needed to hold things together. The temple symbolizes return to shared origins, collective memory, and moral authority. By sincere reverence, scattered hearts may be reunited. “Crossing the great river” shows that danger can be traversed if one uses the right means and structure. Yet the hexagram ends with the importance of perseverance in what is right, reminding us that dissolution must serve renewal, not chaos.
imageDetailZh: 《象传》曰：“风行水上，涣；先王以享于帝立庙。”风吹水面，水纹四散，无定一形，这正是涣的自然图景。风无形而能入，水流动而善变，两者相遇，表现出一种以柔化刚、以动解结的力量。象意提示我们，很多僵局并不是靠硬碰硬打破，而是靠渗透、疏导与感召来化开。先王观此之象，明白在离散之时，最重要的是建立人心所归，所以通过祭天立庙，把分散的情感、意志与秩序重新汇于一处。此象也说明，真正的治理不是一味收紧，而是先让郁塞之气散开，再使其回归有序。
imageDetailEn: The Image says: “Wind moves over water: Dispersion. Thus the ancient kings offered sacrifice to the Lord and established temples.” Wind across water creates ripples that spread outward without fixed form—this is the natural picture of Huan. Wind is invisible yet penetrating; water is fluid and responsive. Together they describe a force that softens rigidity and unties knots through movement rather than collision. The image teaches that many deadlocks are not broken by direct confrontation but by subtle influence, clarification, and emotional release. The ancient rulers, seeing this pattern, understood that when people drift apart, they must restore a place of inner return. By sacrifice and temple-building, they gathered scattered loyalties into a common center. The image therefore suggests that wise leadership does not merely tighten control; it first releases congestion and then reorders what has been dispersed.
modernApplicationZh: 事业方面，涣卦提示当团队出现沟通不畅、方向分裂、执行松散时，重点不是先追责，而是先疏通信息、重建共识、明确核心目标。领导者若能居中协调，坦诚解释变动原因，往往能化散为聚。感情方面，此卦多见于冷淡、误会、疏离之后的修复期，宜主动表达、化解心结、恢复信任，但不可流于空谈，必须以真诚和持续行动来重建关系。财运方面，涣并非聚财之象，更强调资金流动、债务梳理、资源重新配置，适合清理旧账、优化结构，不宜执着于表面得失；若能去除低效与浪费，反而有利于后续积累。健康方面，常与情绪郁结、气机不畅、压力积累有关，宜重视睡眠、循环系统与心理状态，通过运动、出汗、放松、谈心等方式疏导身心。总体来说，现代处境中的涣卦，是先解后成、先通后聚，提醒人们把危机视为重新整理与再度整合的机会。
modernApplicationEn: In career matters, Huan appears when a team suffers from poor communication, fragmented direction, or weak execution. The key is not immediate blame, but clearing channels, restoring shared purpose, and reestablishing a reliable center. A leader who explains change honestly and coordinates calmly can turn scattering into renewed unity. In relationships, this hexagram often points to a phase after distance, misunderstanding, or emotional cooling. It encourages sincere communication, the release of resentment, and the rebuilding of trust—not through empty words, but through consistent action. In finances, Huan is not primarily about accumulation; it speaks more of circulation, restructuring, settling obligations, and reallocating resources. It favors cleaning up inefficiency and reducing waste so that future stability can emerge. In health, it often relates to emotional stagnation, stress buildup, or blocked vitality. Rest, perspiration, movement, therapy, and open conversation may all help restore flow. Overall, in modern life Huan teaches a pattern of first releasing, then rebuilding; first clearing, then gathering. Crisis becomes an opportunity for reintegration.
historicalStoryZh: 可借周人建国后“制礼作乐、宗庙维系人心”来理解涣卦精神。天下初定之时，旧部族、旧势力、旧记忆并未完全消失，人心并不天然统一。若只凭武力压服，表面安定，实则离散暗伏。于是周公辅政，重建礼制，明定宗庙、祭祀、分封与名分，使四方之民在共同秩序与共同信仰中重新归附。此正合“王假有庙”之义：当社会出现松动与离心，不是简单收紧，而是回到根本，以文明秩序和精神中心来化解分裂。涣卦因此不仅是“散”，更是有原则地疏导，有中心地重聚。先散其怨与疑，再聚其心与力，国家方能长治。
historicalStoryEn: The spirit of Huan can be illustrated by the Zhou dynasty’s efforts to stabilize the realm through rites, music, and ancestral institutions after the founding of the state. Though political power had changed hands, old loyalties and local identities remained. Mere force could impose temporary order, but it could not truly unite hearts. The Duke of Zhou therefore helped establish ritual structures, ancestral temples, sacrificial norms, ranks, and shared forms of legitimacy. In doing so, he created a moral and symbolic center to which people could return. This reflects the phrase “the king approaches his temple”: when society begins to loosen and drift, the answer is not only control, but renewal at the root—through common meaning, proper order, and trusted institutions. Huan is thus not chaotic scattering, but principled release followed by centered regathering.
relatedHexagramsNote: 涣卦与第60卦“节”前后相承：涣先解散壅塞，使气机流通；节再立分际，使流通不致失序。若只涣不节，则散漫无归；若只节不涣，则拘滞难行。二者合看，正是治理与修身中“先疏后定、先通后制”的完整过程。
references: 《彖传》：涣，亨，刚来而不穷，柔得位乎外而上同。王假有庙，王乃在中也。利涉大川，乘木有功也。
《象传》：风行水上，涣；先王以享于帝立庙。

judgmentEn: Limitation. Success. Galling limitation must not be persevered in.
imageZh: 泽上有水，节；君子以制数度，议德行。
imageEn: Water over lake: the image of Limitation. Thus the superior man creates number and measure, and examines the nature of virtue and correct conduct.
interpretationZh: 节卦象征节制，泽上有水，水量有限。适度节制则亨通，过度苦节则不可持久。
interpretationEn: Jie symbolizes limitation. Water over the lake is finite. Moderate restraint brings success, but bitter limitation cannot endure.

lines.textEn: Nine at the beginning: Not going out of the door and the courtyard is without blame.
lines.interpretationZh: 不出门庭，知所节制，无过失。
lines.interpretationEn: Not going beyond the door and courtyard. Knowing one's limits brings no blame.
lines.detailZh: 初九曰“不出户庭，无咎”，处节之初，宜先收敛审势，不轻举妄动。户庭之内象征最基本的生活边界，此时能守住起点，不因外诱而躁进，便可免咎。此爻强调节制从细微处开始，先管住脚步与欲望，后方能谋远。
lines.detailEn: The first line says, “Not going out of the courtyard. No blame.” At the beginning of limitation, one should first restrain impulse and observe conditions carefully. The courtyard symbolizes one’s immediate boundary. By not rushing outward under temptation, one avoids trouble. Limitation begins with small acts of self-control.

lines.textEn: Nine in the second place: Not going out of the gate and the courtyard brings misfortune.
lines.interpretationZh: 不出门庭，错失时机，过度节制反而凶险。
lines.interpretationEn: Not going beyond the gate brings misfortune. Excessive restraint at the wrong time.
lines.detailZh: 九二曰“不出门庭，凶”，与初九相似却吉凶不同，因为时位已进，不可仍旧过度闭守。该行动时若仍拘泥不前，节便成塞，反失其机。此爻告诫人：节不是退缩，守分也要合时，错过应有的作为便会招致凶咎。
lines.detailEn: The second line says, “Not going out of the gate and courtyard. Misfortune.” Unlike the first line, the time here calls for measured action. Excessive withdrawal becomes obstruction. Limitation must suit the moment; if one clings to caution when movement is required, discipline turns into paralysis and brings misfortune.

lines.textZh: 六三：不节若，则嗟若，无咎。
lines.textEn: Six in the third place: He who knows no limitation will have cause to lament. No blame.
lines.interpretationZh: 不知节制就会有悲叹，但能自省则无咎。
lines.interpretationEn: Without limitation comes lamentation. But self-awareness removes blame.
lines.detailZh: 六三曰“不节若，则嗟若，无咎”，意为若先前不能自我节制，后来难免叹息懊悔；但能由嗟叹而知改，仍可免于大过。此爻显示节制常在人吃过放纵之亏后才被真正理解。过失并非不可救，关键在于是否及时反省。
lines.detailEn: The third line says, “If one is not limiting, one will lament; yet there is no blame.” Failure to exercise restraint leads to regret. Still, if lament becomes self-awareness, correction remains possible. This line teaches that many people only understand limitation after experiencing the cost of excess, but sincere reflection can prevent greater fault.

lines.textEn: Six in the fourth place: Contented limitation. Success.
lines.interpretationZh: 安然接受节制，自然而然地守度，亨通。
lines.interpretationEn: Contented acceptance of limitation. Natural restraint brings success.
lines.detailZh: 六四曰“安节，亨”，说明最理想的节制状态不是痛苦忍耐，而是安于其度、乐守其分。能把规律内化为日常，就不会感到束缚，反而因秩序稳定而通达顺利。此爻是从外在约束走向内在安顿的关键。
lines.detailEn: The fourth line says, “Contented limitation. Success.” This is the ideal state: discipline is no longer bitter but settled and natural. When one has internalized proper measure, order no longer feels oppressive. Instead, it becomes the basis of smooth progress. Limitation here has matured into inner ease.

lines.textEn: Nine in the fifth place: Sweet limitation brings good fortune. Going brings esteem.
lines.interpretationZh: 甘心节制，吉利；前往行事会受到尊崇。
lines.interpretationEn: Sweet limitation brings good fortune. Taking action earns esteem.
lines.detailZh: 九五曰“甘节，吉，往有尚”，居中得正，为全卦主爻。所谓“甘节”是甘心而行节，不以约束为苦，反能从中生出价值与尊重。处领导位置者若能以中道设限、以仁厚施令，则众人愿从，前往必有嘉尚。
lines.detailEn: The fifth line says, “Sweet limitation. Good fortune. Going forward brings esteem.” As the central ruling line, it represents noble moderation. “Sweet limitation” means discipline willingly embraced because it is just, balanced, and beneficial. A leader who sets humane boundaries through the middle way gains trust, and future action earns honor.

lines.textEn: Six at the top: Galling limitation. Perseverance brings misfortune. Remorse disappears.
lines.interpretationZh: 苦涩的节制，坚持下去则凶，但悔恨终会消失。
lines.interpretationEn: Bitter limitation. Persisting brings misfortune, yet remorse eventually disappears.
lines.detailZh: 上六曰“苦节，贞凶，悔亡”，到节之极处，容易走向苛刻自缚。若仍执意守此苦节，则虽自以为正，实则凶险；但若因此而觉悟、消除先前放纵之悔，亦有转机。此爻提醒人，任何好原则一旦过头，都会背离初衷。
lines.detailEn: The top line says, “Bitter limitation. Perseverance brings misfortune. Regret disappears.” At the extreme, limitation becomes harsh and joyless. If one insists on maintaining such severity, misfortune follows even if one believes it righteous. Yet the experience may also dissolve prior regret by forcing realization. The line warns that every good principle becomes harmful when carried to excess.

overviewZh: 第六十卦为节卦，上坎下兑，泽上有水，象征万物流行必须有所界限。节并非单纯压抑，而是在可行、可久、可安的范围内建立秩序，使行为、情感、资源与欲望都有分寸。卦辞言“节：亨。苦节不可贞”，指出适度的节制能够通达顺遂，但若走向刻苦自束、违背人情与现实，则反而失其正道。此卦强调制度与德行并重，不只是外在规则，更是内在自守。人生之中，凡事业规划、财务管理、言语往来、情感投入，若无节度，往往由盛转衰；若能知止有度，则能聚散得宜、进退从容。节卦真正要人学习的，不是苛刻，而是节而有理、节而有情、节而有恒。
overviewEn: Hexagram 60, Limitation, is formed by Water above Lake. The image suggests water contained above a marsh: movement exists, but it must be bounded by measure. Limitation in the Yijing does not mean repression for its own sake. Rather, it points to the wisdom of structure, proportion, and self-restraint that make continuity possible. The Judgment says, “Limitation. Success. Bitter limitation is not fit to be persevered in,” which shows a crucial distinction: healthy discipline creates flow, while harsh, joyless restriction eventually becomes damaging. This hexagram teaches that order should support life, not suffocate it. Rules, habits, emotions, spending, and ambition all require boundaries if they are to remain constructive. Without measure, excess leads to waste, instability, and regret; with measure, one gains clarity, trust, and sustainability. Limitation therefore represents a mature balance between freedom and form, where one acts with awareness of proper timing, suitable degree, and enduring human reality.
judgmentDetailZh: 卦辞“节：亨。苦节不可贞”揭示节卦的核心精神：节制本身可以带来通达，因为适当的边界能够防止混乱，使人各安其位、各守其分；但若把节制推到痛苦、僵化、刻板的程度，就不再合于正道。《彖传》说：“节，亨，刚柔分而刚得中。苦节不可贞，其道穷也。说以行险，当位以节，中正以通。”所谓“刚柔分而刚得中”，说明此卦之所以可亨，在于阴阳分际清楚，而九五刚中得位，能够以中道立法，不偏不激。“说以行险”，指下兑之悦与上坎之险并存，意味着人在险境中若能以和悦接受约束，反而可以平稳渡过。节不是为罚而罚，而是以中正之道调和人欲与时势。真正的节，贵在合度；若失去弹性与仁厚，就会走向“苦节”，终致道路闭塞。
judgmentDetailEn: The Judgment, “Limitation. Success. Bitter limitation is not fit to be persevered in,” defines the heart of this hexagram. Proper limits create success because they prevent disorder and give shape to action. But when limitation turns severe, joyless, or inhuman, it can no longer be sustained. The Tuan Commentary explains: “Limitation brings success because the firm and yielding are differentiated, and the firm obtains the center. Bitter limitation cannot be maintained, for its way comes to exhaustion. Joyfully acting amid danger, each in its place through limitation, with central correctness one attains free course.” This is important. The structure of the hexagram shows not mere restriction but ordered differentiation, with strength in the correct central place. That is why limitation can be fruitful: it is grounded in balance, not extremism. The phrase “joyfully acting amid danger” points to Lake below and Water above—pleasure beneath, risk above. In difficult conditions, one who accepts discipline with clarity and willingness can pass through danger safely. Limitation, then, is not punitive austerity; it is measured governance of life. The moment it becomes bitter compulsion, its path narrows and collapses.
imageDetailZh: 《象传》曰：“泽上有水，节；君子以制数度，议德行。”泽能蓄水，但水若过多则漫溢，若过少则枯竭，因此必须有节。此象说明自然之道并非无边扩张，而是以界限维持生机。君子观此，不仅制定礼制、法度、数量、标准，也反求诸己，衡量德行是否合宜。所谓“制数度”，是让制度有尺度，让资源有配比，让言行有边界；“议德行”则是说节制不能只靠外在强迫，还要以德性判断何者当止、何者当行。节卦之象因此兼具客观规则与主观修养：外有法，内有心。一个人若能像堤岸导水般调节自己的欲望与行动，便能既不放纵，也不枯槁，在有序之中保存活力。
imageDetailEn: The Image says, “Water above the lake: Limitation. Thus the noble one creates measures and standards, and deliberates upon virtue and conduct.” A lake can gather water, yet if water overflows, it floods; if too little remains, it dries out. The image therefore teaches that life depends on boundaries that preserve balance. From this, the noble person establishes measures, proportions, rules, and workable norms. But the text goes further: one also reflects on virtue and conduct. This means true limitation is not only external regulation; it requires inner judgment. Standards without moral discernment become mechanical, while sentiment without standards becomes chaotic. The hexagram joins both: outer form and inner character. Just as embankments guide water without destroying it, wise discipline channels energy rather than suppressing life. The ideal is neither indulgence nor lifeless severity, but a measured order that preserves vitality, trust, and sustainability.
modernApplicationZh: 事业上，节卦提示要重视流程、预算、权限与时间边界。项目推进不能一味扩张，适时收束、明确标准，反而能提高执行效率。感情上，节不是冷淡，而是懂得分寸，不过度索取、不情绪泛滥，让彼此在舒适边界内建立稳定关系；若控制欲太强或过分压抑表达，便成“苦节”，容易伤人。财运上，此卦最宜用于现金流管理、消费节制与风险隔离，适合做长期规划，不利贪快、透支、冲动投资。健康上，节卦提醒作息、饮食、运动都要有规律，尤其戒极端：暴饮暴食或过度节食、拼命工作或长期懈怠，皆失其中。总体而言，现代生活中的节卦不是叫人缩小人生，而是通过制度化、自律化与边界感，把有限资源用在最值得的地方，从而获得更长久的自由与稳定。
modernApplicationEn: In modern life, Hexagram 60 advises disciplined structure rather than blind expansion. In career matters, it emphasizes process, budget control, decision boundaries, and sustainable pacing. A project often succeeds not by doing more, but by defining scope, authority, and timing clearly. In relationships, limitation means healthy boundaries, not emotional coldness: avoid excessive demands, overexposure, or manipulative control. Mutual respect grows when each person knows what to give, what to hold back, and how to remain sincere without overwhelming the other. In finances, this hexagram strongly supports budgeting, cash-flow awareness, restrained consumption, and risk management. It favors steady planning over speculation, debt-fueled ambition, or impulsive investment. In health, the message is moderation in schedule, food, work, and exercise. Extremes—whether indulgence or harsh denial—damage long-term well-being. Overall, Limitation in a contemporary context means using systems, habits, and boundaries to protect what matters most. It does not shrink life; it makes freedom durable by preventing waste, burnout, and imbalance.
historicalStoryZh: 可借大禹治水来会通节卦之义。上古洪水横流，若只是一味堵截，终将决溃成灾；若全然放任，也会生灵涂炭。禹之所以成功，在于“导”而非“塞”，依山川形势疏通水道，使水各得其所。这正是节卦所讲的“制数度”：不是消灭水，而是为水立界；不是压抑生机，而是使其有序。又如周公制礼作乐，也是在社会秩序初定之时，为尊卑、亲疏、进退建立分寸，使天下不至于乱。节之精神，自然层面是治水，政治层面是立礼，修身层面则是克己复中。其要义都在于：有节则通，无节则乱，苦节则穷。
historicalStoryEn: A fitting historical parallel for Hexagram 60 is the story of Yu the Great controlling the floods. When the ancient waters spread across the land, pure blockage would have failed, because pressure without release leads to catastrophe. Yet complete permissiveness would also have meant destruction. Yu succeeded by channeling rather than merely damming: he studied the terrain, opened pathways, and gave the waters proper courses. This embodies the principle of Limitation—setting form without strangling life. The same spirit can be seen in the Duke of Zhou’s establishment of rites and music. Social order was not created through force alone, but by giving rank, conduct, and relationship their proper measure. In both cases, limitation meant appropriate structuring, not cruel repression. Whether in governing water, society, or oneself, the lesson is the same: with measure there is flow, without measure there is chaos, and with bitter overcontrol the way becomes exhausted.
relatedHexagramsNote: 节卦与第59卦涣卦前后相承：涣是离散之后需要重新整合，节则是在整合之后建立边界与法度；又与第61卦中孚相邻，说明外在节制若要长久，最终仍须以内在诚信为根。无信之节易成苛政，有信之节方能化为自律。
